Tulsi.name: Tulsi
Tulsi.uses: Leaves used to make herbal tea for immunity and respiratory relief.

preparation.steps: Boil 6-8 fresh leaves in a cup of water for 5 minutes. Add honey and lemon.
Chew 3-5 fresh leaves daily in the morning.

Tulsi.region: Widely grown across India in home gardens.
Tulsi.description: Holy Basil used in Ayurveda for respiratory infections and immunity support.

Neem.name: Neem
Neem.uses: Antibacterial and antifungal leaves and bark used for skin and detox.

preparation.steps: Grind fresh leaves with water. Apply to affected skin for 15-20 minutes.
Boil neem leaves in water for 10 minutes; drink after cooling (consult practitioner).

Neem.region: Tropical and semi-tropical regions of India.
Neem.description: Powerful detoxifying herb common in traditional remedies.

Ashwagandha.name: Ashwagandha
Ashwagandha.uses: Adaptogen supporting stress relief and vitality.

preparation.steps: Mix 1 tsp powder in warm milk with honey at night.

Ashwagandha.description: Popular adaptogen used for energy, sleep, and stress resilience.

Brahmi.name: Brahmi

preparation.steps: Crush fresh leaves and strain; take 1-2 tsp with water or honey.

Brahmi.description: Nootropic herb for focus, memory and calmness.

Ginger.name: Ginger

preparation.steps: Boil sliced fresh ginger in water 5-7 minutes; add lemon.

Ginger.description: Common kitchen remedy for colds and digestion.

Amla.name: Amla

preparation.steps: Blend fresh amla with water; strain. Take 20-30 ml diluted.

Amla.description: Emblica fruit used in Triphala and tonics.
